title: Eustachian tube dysfunction
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Supports first-presentation hearing assessment, exclusion of wax or acute infection, audiology, sudden or rapid loss, unilateral neurological signs, otalgia, otorrhoea, and immunocompromise review.
red_flags.rationale: Supports muffled or reduced hearing plus ear pain, discharge, and neurological warning documentation without assigning an Eustachian-tube cause.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports first-presentation hearing assessment, exclusion of wax or acute infection, audiology, sudden or rapid loss, unilateral neurological signs, otalgia, otorrhoea, and immunocompromise review.

source.title: Hearing loss in adults: assessment and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.4 — initial, sudden, and urgent assessment

treatment_and_management.text: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.
treatment_and_management.rationale: Supports otoscopy, hearing assessment, tympanometry when indicated, audiology review, and personalised discussion without treatment advice.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.

source.title: Hearing loss in adults: assessment and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.8.1 — audiology assessment, management, and support

referral.text: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.
referral.rationale: Supports persistent or asymmetric hearing, vertigo, otalgia, discharge, abnormal ear findings, and clinician-led referral prompts.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.

source_limitations: Eustachian-tube-dysfunction identity, ear pressure, popping, blocked-ear sensation, flight trigger, fever, middle-ear effusion, tuning-fork tests, pressure-equalisation advice, watchful waiting, fixed review timing, and the combined history draft remain unsupported.
The workflow must not infer Eustachian-tube dysfunction or recommend steroids, antibiotics, pressure manoeuvres, or referral.
Qualified UAE clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
